channel.title: Statistical Analysis on Stéphane Fréchette
channel.description: Recent content in Statistical Analysis on Stéphane Fréchette

item.title: Ottawa weather vs. Ottawa JavaScript Meetup attendance: an R analysis
item.description:
The more you used it, the more you discover great stuff… I wanted to do some analysis with some weather data, but did not want to start searching and scraping sites, then merging and cleansing…you know the drill. So I came across this neat R package named weatherData. weatherData provides functions that help in fetching weather data from websites, documentation found here -> https://ram-n.github.io/weatherData/.

So I got inspired to compare Ottawa weather and Ottawa JavaScript Meetup attendance! Even with only 33 meetups (observations) in the past 3+ years, I thought it would be interesting to see if there any correlation between them? Ottawa weather vs. Ottawa JavaScript Meetup attendance: an R analysis

item.title: Data Analytics with R and SQL Server
item.description:
First Happy New Year and best wishes for 2015!

Yes, Data Analytics with R and SQL Server … As Data and Business Intelligence Architects I personally think we just can’t dismiss or push aside the Data Science “landscape”. Hence that’s why I registered and currently more than half way through the Data Science Specialization on Coursera from the Johns Hopkins University. Even though I have been working with R for a while I wanted to get some kind of recognition, attestation for my skills and also just because I can 😉
